name: Musicals & Theatre
description: Broadway, West End, and the world of stage productions

questions.q: Which Andrew Lloyd Webber musical features the song 'Memory,' sung by a feline character?

source.notes: Based on T.S. Eliot's 'Old Possum's Book of Practical Cats.' Premiered 1981.

questions.q: Which Arthur Miller play is set during the Salem Witch Trials?

source.notes: Published 1953. Written as an allegory for McCarthyism.

questions.q: Which musical features the song 'With a Little Bit of Luck' and is based on Shaw's 'Pygmalion'?

source.notes: Premiered Broadway 1956. Music by Frederick Loewe, lyrics by Alan Jay Lerner.

questions.q: Which punk rock band had their music adapted into the musical 'American Idiot'?

source.notes: Based on their 2004 album. Broadway premiere 2010.

questions.q: Who wrote 'The Importance of Being Earnest,' premiering in 1895?

source.notes: Wilde's most famous play. Subtitle: 'A Trivial Comedy for Serious People.'

questions.q: Which villain in Shakespeare's 'Othello' manipulates Othello into killing his wife?

source.notes: One of Shakespeare's most villainous characters. Manipulates everyone around him.

questions.q: Which Lin-Manuel Miranda musical tells the story of Alexander Hamilton?

source.notes: Broadway premiere 2015. Won 11 Tony Awards. Based on Ron Chernow's biography.

questions.q: In which Broadway musical does Tracy Turnblad try to integrate a TV dance show in 1960s Baltimore?

source.notes: Based on John Waters' 1988 film. Broadway premiere 2002.

questions.q: Which Stephen Sondheim musical features a barber who slashes throats and a pie maker?

source.notes: Full title: 'Sweeney Todd: The Demon Barber of Fleet Street.' Premiered 1979.

questions.q: Which Rodgers & Hammerstein musical is set in the Austrian Alps?

source.notes: Premiered 1959. 1965 film adaptation won Best Picture Oscar.

questions.q: What prestigious awards honor excellence on Broadway?

source.notes: Named after actress Antoinette Perry. Established 1947.

questions.q: In 'Les Misérables,' what is the name of the protagonist who has spent 19 years in prison?

source.notes: Based on Victor Hugo's 1862 novel. The musical premiered in Paris 1980.

questions.q: Which Andrew Lloyd Webber musical is based on a 1910 French novel by Gaston Leroux?

source.notes: Premiered 1986. Longest-running Broadway show until closing in 2023.

questions.q: In which Shakespeare play does a clown named Touchstone appear?

source.notes: A 'fool' character who delivers witty wisdom throughout the play.

questions.q: Which Shakespeare tragedy is traditionally believed to bring bad luck if its name is spoken in a theater?

source.notes: Actors call it 'The Scottish Play.' Saying 'Macbeth' inside a theater requires a purification ritual.

questions.q: Which famous theater in London was Shakespeare's home stage, burned down in 1613, and was rebuilt in 1997?

source.notes: Original burned 1613 during a performance of Henry VIII when a cannon set the thatch roof on fire.

questions.q: Which musical features 'Cell Block Tango' and is based on real 1920s Chicago murders?

source.notes: 1996 revival won Tony, Best Musical Revival. Based on 1926 play about two murderesses.

questions.q: What is the name of the prestigious theater award given in London (the UK's Tony equivalent)?

source.notes: Named after Laurence Olivier. Presented annually by the Society of London Theatre.

questions.q: Which Dickens-based musical features Fagin, the Artful Dodger, and Nancy?

source.notes: By Lionel Bart, based on 'Oliver Twist.' Premiered 1960 in London.

questions.q: Which landmark Broadway musical premiered in 1927 and featured 'Ol' Man River'?

source.notes: Considered the first 'modern musical,' integrating songs with plot. Music by Jerome Kern.
